title: holotome leucocephala
intro: Holotome leucocephala, also known as the Madras thorn or Jathropa, is a tropical tree native to Asia and the Pacific islands, belonging to the Apocynaceae family of plants. The medicinal properties of Holotome leucocephala are primarily attributed to its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called jatrophine, and have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and skin infections. Holotome leucocephala can be prepared as a herbal remedy by drying and processing the seeds, which can be consumed in various forms, including capsules, tablets, or teas, although caution should be taken due to its toxicity.
